plan.user_intent: Create a beginner-level ~20-minute lesson explaining what Machine Learning is, including a PPTX slide deck and a Python file with explanations and example code.

subtasks.instruction: Draft a 20-minute beginner lesson outline for 'What is Machine Learning?' including learning objectives, timing per section, key terms (dataset, features, labels, model, training, inference, overfitting), and a simple real-world example. Use this outline to plan the slide narrative.

Generate a PPTX slide deck (beginner level, ~20 minutes) titled 'What is Machine Learning?'. Include ~10–14 slides: agenda, definition, types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement) at high level, examples, basic workflow, evaluation concept (train/test split, accuracy), overfitting intuition, ethical considerations, and a recap + Q&A. Add simple diagrams using shapes (pipeline, scatterplot concept) and speaker notes with what to say per slide. Export as a .pptx file.

Create a beginner-friendly Python script (.py) that explains ML concepts with comments and runnable examples. Include: (1) generating a toy dataset (e.g., synthetic house prices), (2) training a simple scikit-learn model (LinearRegression or DecisionTree), (3) train/test split, (4) evaluation (MAE/R^2), (5) short overfitting demo (train vs test error), (6) optional plot code guarded by if __name__ == '__main__'. Ensure the file is self-contained and includes installation notes at top.

Package outputs for delivery: provide final PPTX and Python file paths/names, and include a short README-style summary (how to run the Python script, expected output, and how to present the slides in ~20 minutes).

step1_outline.instruction: Provide a non-null lesson outline. Include explicit timing per section (e.g., minute-by-minute or per segment totaling ~20 minutes) and clearly labeled learning objectives for a beginner audience. Also incorporate and define the required key terms (dataset, features, labels, model, training, inference, overfitting) and add a simple real-world example, using the outline to drive a slide narrative.

step3_python_lesson.instruction: Deterministic failure: no .py file was created (py_file_created=false), so the task must fail. Create a self-contained beginner-friendly Python script file (e.g., ml_intro_lesson.py) and provide its contents in output. Include installation notes at the top, extensive inline comments (#) explaining ML concepts, generate a toy dataset, perform train_test_split, train a simple scikit-learn model (LinearRegression or DecisionTree), evaluate with MAE and/or R^2, and demonstrate overfitting by comparing train vs test error. Guard optional plotting code under if __name__ == '__main__':.

step2_pptx.instruction: No PPTX file was created (deterministic failure). Create and export the slide deck as a .pptx file (e.g., result.pptx). Ensure it contains 10–14 slides and includes speaker notes on slides. Then provide metadata/evidence: slide_count and has_speaker_notes=true.

step4_package_delivery.instruction: No delivery_notes/output were provided. Provide a README-style delivery note that includes: (1) the exact filenames/paths to the final .pptx and the Python script, (2) how to run the script (e.g., `python script.py` plus any dependencies), (3) expected outputs/artifacts produced by the script, and (4) a ~20-minute slide presentation plan.

Use this outline to plan the slide narrative.
